extracted.headquarters: 9350 Waxie Way, Suite 520, San Diego, California 92123
extracted.brandBackground: The UPS Store offers shipping, packaging, postal, print, and similar business and communication services.

leadership.name: Sarah Casalan
leadership.background: Appointed President in October 2021, previously serving as Vice President Stores for Crate and Barrel Holdings.
leadership.whyItMatters: Brings extensive retail store leadership experience to the brand.

leadership.name: Brian Dykes
leadership.role: Director, Vice President, Treasurer, and Assistant Secretary
leadership.background: Elected in July 2024, also serving as Executive Vice President and Treasurer for parent company UPS.
leadership.whyItMatters: Ensures direct financial alignment with parent company UPS.

leadership.name: Eric Woltering
leadership.background: Appointed in September 2025, previously serving as CFO for Roadie, Inc. and M&A Transaction Director for UPS.
leadership.whyItMatters: Brings strong corporate finance and M&A experience from UPS subsidiaries.

operationalRisks.title: Out-of-State Dispute Resolution
operationalRisks.description: Disputes must be resolved by mediation and/or litigation only in California.

operationalRisks.title: Supplier Restrictions
operationalRisks.description: Franchisees must purchase or lease items from designated suppliers, which may be more expensive.

extracted.conceptRationale: The concept is a retail business and communication service center providing shipping, postal, and printing services.
extracted.staffingRationale: Requires a full-time on-premises Primary Operator and sufficient trained staff to manage retail operations.

riskReasons: Economics were assessable and no major stress flags triggered.

underwriting.assessment: With $250,000 liquid against a $414,225 mid-point build-out, there is a $164,225 capital gap. An SBA loan or an equity partner is required to do this deal. At mid-cohort revenue, modeled cash flow after debt service is about $51,352/mo before payroll and maintenance — a thin margin of error.

insights.conceptRationale: The concept is a retail business and communication service center providing shipping, postal, and printing services.

benchmark.label: General franchise (uncategorized)
benchmark.dominantRisk: Cost of goods and labor are not disclosed in any FDD — model them explicitly before trusting any margin figure.
benchmark.rampNote: Assume 6–18 months to a mature run-rate.
considerations: Budget COGS and labor explicitly — neither is in the FDD, and together they usually run 45–65% of sales.
Use the Item 20 franchisee list to validate real operating costs and ramp before committing capital.

crossCheck.message: No operating-EBITDA margin is disclosed in Item 19. The 8–20% band below is an industry estimate for this concept — validate it against real franchisee P&Ls (Item 20) before relying on any margin.

buildup.label: − Labor
buildup.note: conventionally staffed · ≈ 3.5–6.1 FTE at ~$20/hr fully loaded

assumptions.detail: Classified as “General franchise (uncategorized)” from the FDD (Item 1 / brand description).
Taken from the FDD (~$6,000/mo); already netted in margin after fees & rent.
Estimated at the 15–35% category band — COGS is never disclosed in an FDD.
Estimated at the 20–35% conventionally staffed band — labor is never disclosed in an FDD.
Utilities, insurance, and R&M estimated at 6–10% (category catch-all).
Our calculation — the disclosed top line minus the benchmarked costs above. Not a figure stated in the FDD.

insights.consultCtaPitch: These are category benchmarks applied to the disclosed top line — your unit's real costs will differ, and that difference is the conversation. Want them pressure-tested against actual franchisee P&Ls for your territory?
insights.asOf: 2026 — general industry benchmarks, refine against real franchisee P&Ls
insights.disclaimer: Industry benchmark ranges for your own budgeting and Item 20 questions — NOT a projection of this franchise's results, and not investment advice.

financialCondition.headline: We couldn't extract enough of this franchisor's financial statements to assess its condition. Request the audited Item 21 financials and have them reviewed before committing.
body: Why it matters to you: field support, training, technology, and marketing come out of the franchisor's budget. If outside funding slows, those are the first things to get cut. There is no parent guarantee of performance in this FDD, so United Parcel Service, Inc. is not contractually obligated to keep funding the franchisor or backing its commitments to you.

Verify before you commit: ask for the most recent interim (unaudited) financials and the current-year funding plan; ask whether the parent will sign a guarantee of performance; and cross-check Item 20 — unit closures, transfers, and terminations are the operational tell that corroborates financial stress.
aggravators: no parent guarantee backing the franchisor's obligations to you
mitigants: the auditor issued a clean (unmodified) opinion and did not raise going-concern doubt
financialCondition.evidenceNote: Derived from the Item 21 / Exhibit F audited financials. Figures are extracted; all ratios and grading are computed. Confirm against the source statements and a CPA before relying on them.
